app.name: Keymano
action.saveTooltip: Save changes to the current file. New layouts ask where to save; this never alters an installed keyboard.
action.saveAsTooltip: Save a copy to a new file.
installed.title: Import from system
installed.hint: Editable layout files open directly. Apple's built-in input sources are sealed and can't be read by any app — picking one starts a blank layout you can build.
installed.empty: No installed layout files found. macOS ships its built-in layouts in a sealed bundle that can't be exported — open a .keylayout file instead.
installed.uninstallMessage: “{{name}}” will be moved to the Trash. You can restore it from there.
installed.forkedDesc: Apple seals its built-in layouts, so they can't be imported. This is an empty layout to build from — to edit a real one, open its .keylayout file.
template.fromSystem.desc: Open a keyboard already installed on this Mac.
template.basic.desc: Empty Unicode layout with the standard modifier set.
template.standard.desc: Prefilled US QWERTY base — ready to tweak.
about.title: About
about.author: Author
help.label: Help — guided tour
help.nudge: New here? Click the “?” for a quick tour of this page.
tour.welcome.intro.title: Welcome to Keymano
tour.welcome.intro.body: Design and edit macOS keyboard layouts. Start from a template, open a .keylayout/.bundle file, or fork one already installed on this Mac.
tour.welcome.tiles.title: Pick a starting point
tour.welcome.tiles.body: “Standard (US)” gives you a ready-to-tweak QWERTY base. “Basic” is an empty Unicode layout. “From system” forks a layout installed on this Mac.
tour.welcome.open.title: Open existing files
tour.welcome.open.body: Open a .keylayout or .bundle from disk — or just drag one anywhere onto the window.
tour.editor.keyboard.title: The keyboard
tour.editor.keyboard.body: Each key shows its current output. Click a key to select it, double-click (or use Edit) to change what it types. Right-click for copy/paste, unlink, and dead-key options.
tour.editor.modbar.title: Modifier layers
tour.editor.modbar.body: Toggle Shift, Option, etc. to edit that modifier combination. Latched modifiers stay on so you can edit a whole layer.
tour.editor.kbtype.title: Physical layout
tour.editor.kbtype.body: Switch between ANSI, ISO, and JIS key geometries. The layout’s data is shared; only the physical shape changes.
tour.editor.tools.title: Editing tools
tour.editor.tools.body: Edit a key, Unlink it from the base map (make it absolute), Relink it back, or Swap two keys. Quick Entry lets you type characters straight onto keys, advancing automatically.
tour.editor.find.title: Find a key
tour.editor.find.body: Search by the character a key outputs, or jump straight to a key by its hardware code.
tour.editor.export.title: Export
tour.editor.export.body: Save a PNG of the current view, or a monochrome reference sheet covering every modifier map — handy for printing.
tour.editor.inspector.title: Inspector
tour.editor.inspector.body: Details of the selected key: code, output, code points, whether it’s dead or inherited from the base map.
tour.modifiers.map.title: Modifier map
tour.modifiers.map.body: Each row maps a physical modifier combination to a keymap index. “any” matches either side (left/right); “?” marks an optional modifier. This is how the layout decides which key map to use.
tour.deadkeys.intro.title: Dead keys & actions
tour.deadkeys.intro.body: Dead keys enter a state (e.g. acute) and the next keystroke completes it (á). Edit each state’s terminator — the character produced if nothing follows.
tour.deadkeys.housekeeping.title: Housekeeping
tour.deadkeys.housekeeping.body: Remove unused states/actions to keep the layout clean, or add the standard special-key outputs that macOS expects.
tour.bundle.intro.title: Bundle
tour.bundle.intro.body: A .bundle can hold several layouts with shared metadata (identifier, name, version). Edit the bundle details here, then export to install all layouts at once.
tour.xml.intro.title: XML & validation
tour.xml.intro.body: Live preview of the generated .keylayout XML. Toggle whether non-ASCII output is encoded as code points, and copy the XML out.
tour.xml.validation.title: Validation
tour.xml.validation.body: Issues that would stop macOS from loading the layout show here. Auto-repair fixes the common ones (missing special keys, bad ids, keymap gaps).
welcome.dropHint: Drop a .keylayout or .bundle here to open.
confirm.closeMessage: This layout has unsaved edits. Close it and lose them?
confirm.quitMessage: One or more layouts have unsaved edits. Quit anyway?
status.exportSheet: Export a reference sheet (all modifier maps, monochrome PNG)
toast.downloadedDesc: Your browser can't write to ~/Library/Keyboard Layouts. The .keylayout was downloaded — move it there, then add it in System Settings → Keyboard → Input Sources.
toast.installedDesc: Saved to ~/Library/Keyboard Layouts. Re-add it in System Settings → Keyboard → Input Sources (or log out/in).
splash.tagline: A classic instrument for macOS keyboard layouts
error.deadStateEdit: Switch the dead state back to 'none' before editing a key
error.deadStateClear: Switch the dead state back to 'none' before clearing a key
a11y.keyboard: Interactive {{kbType}} keyboard — arrow keys to move, Enter to edit
